note: Auditable view-coverage decision table for the kids' two-step step stool (task STOOLBUILD). Each candidate area is scored on (1) installation/fabrication complexity and (2) difficulty of seeing it from the primary whole-model views, then decided ZOOM or a recorded WHY-NOT. Audited BOTH directions: a high-complexity, poorly-visible area with no zoom and no why-not is a finding; a redundant zoom (mirror/simple/well-visible) is over-generation and is a why-not here. Renders: scripts/render_stool_views.py -> outputs/step_stool/views/.
context_treatment: The floor is a role:existing context body; more importantly the SOLID side panels occlude the two-step interior in plain side/oblique views (finding V1). The hero + zoom variants are rendered with the floor (and where needed the near panel) HIDDEN so the treads, cleats and joinery read.

primary_views.shows: whole stool on the floor (solid panels occlude the interior; see g1_iso floor-hidden hero)
both step surfaces (5.5in lower + 10.25in upper) and the footprint width
depth + front/back tread stagger (near panel occludes; see g1_iso)
both treads and the screw pattern from above

candidate_areas.area: two-step interior (tread stagger, toe room, both heights)
candidate_areas.rationale: the solid side panels occlude the staggered treads in every plain oblique/side primary (finding V1); the floor-hidden iso is the one view where the whole two-step form reads, so it is the document hero.

candidate_areas.area: lower-tread cleat joint (1x2 cleat_screwed to the panel face; tread on cleat top)
candidate_areas.view: z_cleat.png (from below, near panel + far cleat hidden)
candidate_areas.rationale: highest local density — a fastening spot (cleat_screwed, 2 screws) AND a separate declared tread-on-cleat bearing — and fully tucked under the tread against the inner face; only an interior/below angle with the near panel hidden makes the cleat and its screw heads read.
mirror-identical to the +X cleat joint about the Y-Z plane; one representative zoom suffices. A second identical zoom would be over-generation.

candidate_areas.area: upper-tread rail_cap joint (tread capping the panel top, screwed down)
candidate_areas.rationale: the load-bearing gravity seat + the child's-grab lateral path; the top primary shows the screw stations but the seat of the tread on the panel end reads best in the corner zoom.

candidate_areas.area: governing step-height callouts (5.5in lower, 10.25in upper) + footprint depth
candidate_areas.rationale: the two heights are the pick's contract and are dimensioned by the derived callouts overlaid on the FRONT/SIDE primaries + asserted in the model's dimension checks; a dedicated zoom would add nothing the callouts do not.

candidate_areas.area: panel-to-floor bearing (each panel foot flat on the floor)
candidate_areas.rationale: a single flush bearing plane per panel, no fabrication; the FRONT/ISO primaries show the panels standing on the floor. A dedicated zoom would add nothing.

no toe-kick FEATURE is authored (residual R2 — no edge-rebate FEATURE verb; the open front already gives toe room), so there is no fabricated geometry to zoom; the absence is the fact, recorded here rather than drawn.

interior geometry with no section/cutaway in this render path; the embedment is VALIDATED (hardware-presence PASS + allowlisted screw-into-wood overlaps), not drawn. An honest render limitation, not a fabricated cutaway.
